content.problemStatement: You are given three integers NNN, KKK and XXX. Is it possible to find a sequence of non-negative integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ such that the number of pairs of indices (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) (1≤i≤j≤N1 \le i \le j \le N1≤i≤j≤N) satisfying Ai⊕Aj=XA_i \oplus A_j = XAi​⊕Aj​=X is equal to KKK? Here, ⊕\oplus⊕ is the bitwise XOR operator.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, KKK and XXX.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing the string `"YES"` if it is possible to find a sequence satisfying the given condition or `"NO"` if it is impossible (without quotes).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: For example, A=[2,2,1,2]A = [2, 2, 1, 2]A=[2,2,1,2] satisfies the given condition, since there are exactly 333 pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that Ai⊕Aj=3A_i \oplus A_j = 3Ai​⊕Aj​=3.
Example case 3: For example, A=[8,12,3,14,8,15,10,10,8,14]A = [8, 12, 3, 14, 8, 15, 10, 10, 8, 14]A=[8,12,3,14,8,15,10,10,8,14] satisfies the given condition, since there are exactly 888 pairs with XOR equal to 222.
